question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a stored memo field to contain valid UTF-8 but with embedded Unicode control characters or bidirectional override characters, given that ByteString.copyFromUtf8(addressBookEntry.getMemo()) performs no sanitization, triggering delivery of visually spoofed node metadata to clients, causing clients to misread node information and connect to wrong endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a stored description field to contain an extremely long string (e.g., 100MB), given that setDescription(addressBookEntry.getDescription()) has no length limit in toNodeAddress(), triggering allocation of a massive protobuf string field per node, causing heap exhaustion when multiple nodes with large descriptions are streamed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onErrorMap(ProtoUtil::toStatusRuntimeException) error handler by crafting a request that triggers a NonTransientDataAccessResourceException, given that serverError() logs the full exception with log.error('Server error: ', t), triggering verbose server-side logging of internal database errors, causing log-based information disclosure of database schema or query structure?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a stored ipAddressV4 to be an IPv6 address string like '::1' or 'fe80::1', given that InetAddress.getByName() accepts both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses and toIpAddressV4() does not enforce IPv4-only input, triggering getAddress() to return a 16-byte IPv6 address instead of a 4-byte IPv4 address, causing clients to misparse the byte array as an IPv4 address and connect to wrong endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a stored nodeCertHash to be a zero-length byte array, given that ProtoUtil.toByteString() returns ByteString.EMPTY for null but not for empty arrays, triggering setNodeCertHash(ByteString.EMPTY) which signals 'no certificate' to clients, causing clients to skip certificate validation and connect to nodes without verifying their TLS certificates?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit concurrent AddressBookQuery streams with limit=Integer.MAX_VALUE from multiple connections simultaneously, given that no global concurrency limit or per-IP connection cap is visible in the controller, triggering parallel unbounded database result streaming, causing database CPU and I/O exhaustion leading to complete service outage?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an AddressBookQuery with a FileID containing a valid but unexpected file number (e.g., fileNum=999999), given that toFilter() does not whitelist allowed file IDs, triggering a database query for a non-existent address book file, causing an EntityNotFoundException that leaks the internal entity ID format through the error message returned to the client?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Mono.fromCallable(() -> toFilter(request)) pattern by submitting a request that causes toFilter() to throw a RuntimeException not caught by onErrorMap, given that only specific exception types are mapped in ProtoUtil.toStatusRuntimeException(), triggering an unhandled exception that propagates through the reactive pipeline, causing the gRPC stream to terminate with an opaque error that leaks internal state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a stored serviceEndpoint to have both domainName and ipAddressV4 set simultaneously, given that toNodeAddress() sets both fields without checking for mutual exclusivity as required by the Hedera protobuf spec, triggering delivery of ambiguous ServiceEndpoint messages to clients, causing clients to use the wrong connection target and potentially connect to attacker-controlled infrastructure?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a stored serviceEndpoint domainName to be an empty string while ipAddressV4 is also empty, given that toNodeAddress() calls setDomainName(s.getDomainName()) and toIpAddressV4() without requiring at least one valid endpoint, triggering delivery of a ServiceEndpoint with no usable connection information, causing clients to fail to connect to nodes and disrupting transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an AddressBookQuery with a valid fileId but with the gRPC deadline set to an extremely large value, given that no server-side deadline enforcement is visible in the controller, triggering a long-lived streaming connection that holds server resources indefinitely, causing resource exhaustion through connection slot starvation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a stored ipAddressV4 to be a multicast address like '224.0.0.1', given that toIpAddressV4() calls InetAddress.getByName() without filtering multicast ranges, triggering delivery of a multicast address to clients, causing clients to attempt connections to multicast groups instead of unicast node endpoints, disrupting transaction routing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a stored publicKey to be an empty string or whitespace, given that toNodeAddress() calls setRSAPubKey(addressBookEntry.getPublicKey()) only when publicKey != null but does not check for empty/blank strings, triggering delivery of an empty RSA public key to clients, causing clients to fail signature verification or skip it entirely when connecting to nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the subscribe(responseObserver::onNext, responseObserver::onError, responseObserver::onCompleted) pattern by triggering an error mid-stream, given that onErrorMap maps errors to StatusRuntimeException and calls responseObserver::onError, triggering the client to receive a partial node list followed by an error, causing the client to use an incomplete and potentially misleading address book for transaction routing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an AddressBookQuery where the FileID's shardNum differs from the expected shard, given that toFilter() calls EntityId.of(query.getFileId()) without validating the shard matches the network's shard, triggering a cross-shard address book query, causing the server to return address book data from a different shard context or an unexpected error that leaks shard configuration?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a stored nodeId to be a negative long value, given that toNodeAddress() calls setNodeId(addressBookEntry.getNodeId()) without range validation, triggering delivery of a negative node ID to clients, causing clients to reject the node entry or misroute transactions due to invalid node identification?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a stream of AddressBookQuery requests with incrementing fileId values to enumerate all valid address book file IDs, given that getNodes() returns EntityNotFoundException for invalid IDs (leaking existence information) versus data for valid IDs, triggering a binary search or linear scan over file ID space, causing complete enumeration of all address book files stored in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a stored nodeCertHash to be a crafted byte sequence that is a valid hash of an attacker-controlled certificate, given that toNodeAddress() delivers nodeCertHash without any signature or chain-of-trust verification, triggering clients to accept the attacker's certificate hash as authoritative, causing clients to establish TLS connections to attacker-controlled nodes believing them to be legitimate?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an AddressBookQuery with limit=1 followed immediately by limit=Integer.MAX_VALUE in alternating requests, given that each request creates a new Mono/Flux subscription with no shared state or rate limiting, triggering oscillating database load patterns, causing query planner cache thrashing and degraded database performance for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ServerCallStreamObserver cancel handler by rapidly opening and canceling streams, given that setOnCancelHandler(disposable::dispose) disposes the reactive subscription but does not prevent immediate reconnection, triggering repeated subscription creation and disposal at high frequency, causing thread pool starvation in the reactive scheduler?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a stored serviceEndpoint domainName to contain a string exceeding the maximum DNS label length (253 characters), given that setDomainName() is called without length validation, triggering delivery of an invalid domain name to clients, causing clients to fail DNS resolution and be unable to connect to any nodes, resulting in transaction submission failure?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a stored ipAddressV4 to be a broadcast address like '255.255.255.255', given that toIpAddressV4() calls InetAddress.getByName() without filtering broadcast addresses, triggering delivery of a broadcast address to clients, causing clients to send transactions to the broadcast address and exposing transaction content to all hosts on the network segment?
